notes.anchor: If you are well, it is well
notes.note: Renders the conventional epistolary opening formula "si vales, bene est" (abbreviated S. v. b.); a standard polite health-wish at the head of a Roman letter.

notes.anchor: asked something by edict of a consul
notes.note: As praetors, Brutus and Cassius could issue edicts of their own; they protest that Antony, a mere fellow magistrate, treats their edict requesting safe-conduct as an affront and answers it with threats of arms.

notes.anchor: The day before the nones of Sextilis
notes.note: Sextilis was the old Roman name for the month later renamed August in honour of Augustus; its Nones fell on 5 August, so this dates the letter to 4 August 44 BC.
